methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Safe Superintelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $32B

private.name: Physical Intelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B+

private.name: Nominal

private.name: Photoroom
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Air Space Intelligence is a Series B-stage AI & Software company founded by Lucas Kukielka that has raised $50M+ to date. The comparable universe consists of five same-sector, same-stage private peers (Safe Superintelligence at $32.0B, Physical Intelligence at $5.6B, World Labs at $5.0B, Nominal at $1.0B, and Photoroom at $500M), alongside one public comparable (Palantir Technologies).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy base of $50M and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yields $95M. Peer-median analysis across the five same-stage, same-sector comparables produces a median valuation of $5.0B, with a range spanning $500M to $32.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in business model maturity and market traction across the peer set.

Triangulation of these two methods implies a valuation range of $81M (low) to $5.8B (high), with a median of $2.5B. The stage-progression method anchors conservatively near the low end, while peer medians dominate the upper half. Key uncertainties include the appropriate weighting of outlier peers (particularly Safe Superintelligence's $32B valuation) and whether Air Space Intelligence's commercial traction and technology differentiation align more closely with earlier-stage or later-stage peers in the cohort.
